title: Linear B
description: Reading about Linear B, the oldest Greek dialect. Notes on the Mycenaeans, inflected languages, the first Olympics, and a photo of my notebook where the Greek is creeping in under the Dutch.

title: The Minoan Civilization
description: Learning languages pulled me into history. Just me reading about the Minoans on Crete and writing down whatever I found cool.

title: The Library of Babel
description: Brain fried from math, so instead I wrote about the library that holds every book that could ever be written, this blog included. The thought that keeps me up at night.

title: You Can Actually Think
description: Not about lost jobs or AI slop. At the end, all I am saying is: you can actually think.

title: Offloading and the Compression of Knowledge
description: Older Mathstodon posts. On offloading digestion to fire, cognition to silicon, and proof writing quietly becoming a hobby.

title: Life Doesn't Operate in Extremes
description: Older Mathstodon posts. Why I think Tegmark's Life 3.0 is way too black and white.

title: Quantized Mind
description: A thought that math does not flow, it jumps, and self-reference is always the thing that forces the jump.

title: By Design
description: A half formed theory I wrote mindlessly to climb out of my first research block. The laws quietly keeping us pointed at the purpose.

title: The Dvole Cycle
description: Something I wrote a while back. A theory about intelligence, clutter, and why more capable AI is not the threat everyone thinks it is.

title: Borderline Society and Existentials
description: On reading a lot lately, the project I won't name, and why understanding the universe might be the one purpose that never changed.
